body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Instead of having two types `PrognosticSoilConditions` and `PrognosticGroundConditions`, use `PrognosticGroundConditions` anytime the canopy is run as part of an integrated model. Dispatch on `prognostic_land_components` instead wherever necessary.

closes #1272

## Content
- [x] in ground albedo functions, dispatch on `prognostic_land_components` instead of `PrognosticXConditions` type
- [x] use `PrognosticGroundConditions` instead of `PrognosticSoilConditions` for SoilCanopyModel


<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: remove PrognosticSoilConditions

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Generalize diagnostic reductions instead of only exposing averages to users, and remove hard-coded functions for averaging. Instead, dispatch on user-provided Symbols for `reduction_period` and `reduction_type` to create `ScheduledDiagnostic`s more generally and reduce code duplication.


## To-do
- [x] rename `average_period` -> `reduction_period`
- [x] add an argument `reduction_type` to `default_diagnostics`
- [x] instead of if/else branches in `default_diagnostics`, call `common_diagnostics(reduction_period, reduction_type, output_writer, start_date, short_names, ...)
- [x] add a common_diagnostics method that uses helper functions to dispatch on Vals to parse the available periods (monthly, weekly, daily, hourly, halfhourly) and reductions (average, max, min, inst)
  - `get_period(::Val{:monthly}) = Month(1)`, etc
  - `get_reduction(::Val{:average}) = (+)` (and pass `average_pre_output_hook!`), etc
- [x] remove hard-coded functions like `monthly_averages`, etc
- [x] update docs
- [x] verify that plots are unchanged


<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: generalize diagnostic reductions

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
Add C4 photosynthesis support to the pmodel; closes #1329.

the LHF and GPP for the Ozark pmodel test look indistinguishable between main branch and here. (C3 photosynthesis)

## To-do
Calibrate quantum yield parameters. Different sets are reported in the literature, due to different definitions and possibly to different values also.
 φ_0 (T)=-0.008+0.00375∙T-0.58∙e^(-4)∙T^2, for C4; supplement of https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/epdf/10.1029/2024MS004599. they reference Scott and Smith

what is ACTUALLY in Scott and Smith: φ_0 (T)=-0.0805+0.022∙T-0.00034∙T^2
They say that this is the same as for C3. 

Stocker et al 2020: for C3, φ_0 (T)= 0.087*(0.352+0.022∙T-0.00034∙T^2) - Stocker 2020.

Care must be taken to compare with Bonan's book! He uses different definitions, which are also what we use in the Farquhar model
title: Add C4 plants for P-model
